item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the best thing in this is the implication that the anomaly is aware of a bigger picture of itself out there, but the foundation just can't find it for some reason. A lot could be done with that concept. But the idea of the foundation panicking about ordinary fat people is pretty dumb imo. :T

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mmmmmm nooo. Obesity is caused by aliens? No. I'm sorry, if that implication wasn't in the article, I might've upvoted, but belittling a problem like that that is spurred on by real world issues? Mmm.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neutral voting for precisely this reason. It's executed well, but this "type" of SCP feels over-done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Trying to resist the urge to downvote because I'm bitter… wonder if I have one of these things… let my own views about size acceptance get in the way of good writing… repeatedly tell myself "you're not that big, don't worry" because the person in this article was smaller than me when she got her talking fat cells… lose my objectivity.

I can't really see anything too wrong with this, I suppose. I just… can't bring myself to read the comments on this thing. No vote for now, but I can't really give a better reason… sorry, wish I could have been more help. I'll give you this, though: that is a hell of a clever title.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Have to echo the fact that the article is just silliness mixed in with a tiny bit of body horror (but not much) and a wham line tacked onto the very end, long after I stopped being interested. I don't actually mind the silliness, but that's all there is to it for me to not be able to like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this article. I like that the host is slowly being taken control of completely and that this is a very medically sound document. I know a lot of people are complaining that this SCP seems a bit too wacky and that it's obsession with TV is just lame but I want to give my headcanon as to what happened.

I feel like this SCP was originally meant for a lot more. I feel like that maybe it could have started as simply something to learn and understand us to assimilate with us and then take over. This one may have been one of the first few trials. After infecting the host it begins to gain sentience and taking in everything from the perspective of the host. Eventually it started learning from TV but maybe the first host was a very avid TV watcher of the aforementioned shows in the document. So instead of learning and gaining useful knowledge it gained addiction. Addiction to TV. Eventually it adapted a personality of a general person it might have seen on its original host's favorite TV show.

That is a nutshell of how I see this entire ordeal. I feel like this SCP COULD have been keter and world ending and unstoppable but instead it succumbed to human addiction and compulsion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whoo boy. You new around here? Cuz fat fanciers would be about the most normal fetish for people who like their scips mixed with porn. Don't go look, you'll be traumatized for life, try the forums here if you must, it's all hidden in links and collapsibles, cuz pony Able is a thing.

But yeah, add me to the group who was liking it until the implication that overweight kids have scip parasite things and don't need to go play outside, cut out the soda, or whatever other sort of proactive approach might work for them. Nope, nothing they can do, just passive hosts for this thing. The idea that fat people lack agency does not sit well with me. It was good, and then it played into negative cultural troupes and blah, no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
..What.

Seriously.. What.

Neutral vote, just because i'm just having the idea of a group of bizzare people with equally bizzare fetishes coming from the dark corners of the internet to fap to this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is basically a good idea effectively wasted on a "Man this scip is sooooooo wacky!" premise. It's a major let down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This struck me as trying to make some kind of clumsy social commentary on sedentary lifestyles and obesity, which made the whole thing feel a little too preachy to me, especially the section after the logs, regarding obesity rates among children.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like most of this. My main criticism is that the Foundation either doesn't have a complete long term plan, or else their goals aren't spelled out more clearly in the containment procedures. Presumably they want the host to live as long as possible - so they control diet and mandate exercise. But they must also believe that if the host lost too much weight, that might destroy the skip. The handlers must have some idea of the optimal risk balance between host death due to bad health and skip death due to good health.

The procedures do suggest there's a balancing act, but I think there's an opportunity to heighten the horror by a) documenting the optimal (awful) health conditions with more precision, and b) hint that one reason they have to amnesticize the host on a regular basis is to prevent the diet and exercise from becoming a good habit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This sounds like an over-elaborate yo-mama joke. "Yo Mama so fat, her flab became self-aware" or the like. Well written, though; +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic. I like the body horror, I like the 'wacky' personality, and I like the little tidbits thrown in at the end, leaving it totally wide open.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with some of the other critiques posted about the direction that it was taken, but I was just amused/horrified enough to give it the slight upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-2611-1 is a sentient mass of fat cells located on the left flank of SCP-2611"

Is that the correct use of the term "flank"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, does this thing have a face, or does it just control the poor woman completely? I guess I must have missed something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While reading the interviews, I could not shake the mental image of SCP-2611-1 just being TroyL, which made this all the more entertaining.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These are also my feels. So much wasted potential here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So fat people may have an SCP 2611 inside them

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to know why it is given TV to placate it. It is just as simple to lock it in a room without television, and since its host clearly isn't amicable with its situation, no reason to do that.

I feel as though you could have done more with this idea, but turning it into a couch potato isn't terribly interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know. I mean the premise is interesting enough but it doesn't really hit me the right way. Nothing technically wrong either. An anomalous explanation for obesity? No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't have strong enough feelings about this to vote one way or another. Like others, I'm a little nonplussed about the direction you chose to take it: television addiction rather than some other, more interesting article focus. But the form of the piece is nigh immaculate, and the clinical tone of the description is spot on.

One thing that edges me very nearly toward the upvote is the implication that this entity is one of many million currently inhabiting what appear, at first, to be mundane obese humans. Which at first seems clever, but in the end basically amounts to "people don't get fat on their own; alien parasites make them fat"… I don't know whether to be mildly offended or just laugh at the narminess of it.

Good execution saves it, though. If I were you, I'd consider (although probably never implement) a re-write with a more creative or psychological focus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not too keen on this, mainly because this isn't the sequel to Unfinished Business II we already have some talking tumors and other body parts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same. The idea of a sentient fat mass forcing the host to eat and laze around to make itself bigger is good, but I think giving it the personality of an obnoxious vidiot detracts from the article. Neutral vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My downvote is pretty much a matter of personal preference. I would have rather seen the potential here for some truly uncomfortable body horror and exploration of psychological trauma, as opposed to more Series 3 silliness. But that's just me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ohhh. Ohhhhhhhhh man. This is really cool. Starts of simple, ends with cool interesting implications. I think this might be one of my new favorite SCPs.

item.title: Upvoted.
item.content:encoded: As commented in chat, this is cool. It does fit perfectly well in the THIS IS WEIRD SCP fiction, and I have no medical complaints. :3
